title: Sheinbaum Reaffirms 'Gulf of Mexico' Name Amid Renewed Trump Administration Pressure
detail: At a public event in Sombrerete, Zacatecas, President Sheinbaum reaffirmed Mexico's official position on the name of the Gulf of Mexico, stating: 'It is called the Gulf of Mexico, not the Gulf of America.' Sheinbaum acknowledged wanting to maintain working relations with Washington — 'We don't want any problems with the U.S. government, we are partners' — but said the renaming demand was unacceptable after the Trump administration repeated its 'Gulf of America' framing. The naming dispute has become a symbol of broader sovereignty friction between Mexico City and Washington. Mexico has used 'Gulf of Mexico' since 1607, and the name is established by the UN and IHO under maritime law.

title: Estadio Azteca Reopens as Estadio Banorte After World Cup Renovations; Mexico Draws Portugal 0-0
detail: The iconic Estadio Azteca — newly renamed Estadio Banorte under a naming-rights deal — reopened to the public after approximately two years of World Cup renovation works. Mexico hosted Portugal in an international friendly, drawing 0-0 in front of a packed stadium. Portugal controlled 61% possession; Gonçalo Ramos hit the post. Mexico's Israel Reyes earned Man of the Match. Cristiano Ronaldo was absent with a hamstring injury. One fan died after falling from the VIP box area. Protesters gathered outside, demonstrating over Mexico's 130,000+ missing persons and the stadium's costly renovation budget. Sheinbaum visited Guadalajara the same day to pledge a 100,000-strong joint security force for the June 2026 FIFA World Cup across Mexico, U.S., and Canada, meeting with security chiefs from 20+ federal agencies.
